stimulus: Tiny biological motors—flagella—offer cells a remarkably efficient method of propulsion through fluids, since rotating these whip-like filaments can drive a bacterium forward ______ in some cases, allow it to reverse course in milliseconds.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. This choice uses paired punctuation in the form of two commas to set off the nonessential phrase "in some cases."

Choice A is incorrect. The phrase "in some cases" is a nonessential element and needs to be set off with paired punctuation: a comma is also needed after "and" to match the one after "cases." Choice B is incorrect. To set off the nonessential phrase, a dash would also be needed after "and" to match the one following "cases." Choice D is incorrect. A comma would also be needed after "cases" to match the one following "and" and complete the paired punctuation.
